display.purpose: Hero headline only. One per page maximum.

editorial.purpose: Instrument Serif italic. Reserved for one editorial moment per page — usually inside the hero.

body.purpose: Default paragraph text. Max line length 65–75ch.

label.purpose: Eyebrow labels, button text, form labels. +0.04em letter-spacing — the institutional-voice signal.

mono.purpose: Transaction IDs, rate breakdowns, contract reference numbers. Never default body.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default easing for scroll reveals and translateY hover lifts.

motion.name: marquee
motion.purpose: Continuous-scroll trust marquee. Pauses on hover, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

motion.name: live-pulse
motion.purpose: Live indicator on numbers strip and active-shoots feed only.

motion.name: shimmer-slide
motion.purpose: Inner shimmer sweep on the Shimmer Button. One CTA per page maximum.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The platform's pill-shaped CTA. Infrastructure Blue on canvas, transparent border that becomes the focus ring.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 36px; padding: 0 14px; border-radius: 26px; border: 1px solid transparent; background: #3a8dc4; color: oklch(0.98 0.006 240); font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), transform 120ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #2f77a8; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #3a8dc4; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(58, 141, 196, 0.5); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Shimmer Button
components.description: The page's signature CTA. Ink-on-canvas pill with a Set Light shimmer sweep. One per page only.
components.css: .ds-btn-shimmer { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 44px; padding: 0 24px; border-radius: 26px; border: 1px solid transparent; background: #0d1b26; color: oklch(0.98 0.006 240); font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; overflow: hidden; cursor: pointer; isolation: isolate; transition: transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-shimmer__label { position: relative; z-index: 2; } .ds-btn-shimmer__sweep { position: absolute; inset: 0; background: linear-gradient(90deg, transparent 0%, rgba(249, 162, 108, 0.35) 35%, rgba(230, 126, 62, 0.6) 50%, rgba(249, 162, 108, 0.35) 65%, transparent 100%); transform: translateX(-100%); animation: ds-shimmer-sweep 3s ease-in-out infinite alternate; z-index: 1; } .ds-btn-shimmer:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); } @keyframes ds-shimmer-sweep { to { transform: translateX(100%); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-btn-shimmer__sweep { animation: none; opacity: 0; } }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Transparent at rest. Used inside tables, dense UI, and secondary navigation.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 36px; padding: 0 12px; border-radius: 26px; border: 1px solid transparent; background: transparent; color: #0d1b26; font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(0.955 0.01 240); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #3a8dc4; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(58, 141, 196, 0.5); }

components.name: Flat Card
components.description: Flat surface with a hairline ring. No drop shadow, ever. Lifts 2px on hover when interactive.
components.html:
SHOOT • EDITORIAL
Spring/Summer Lookbook
London · 16 Jun · £680 + agreed hours
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; padding: 24px; border-radius: 18px; background: oklch(0.98 0.006 240); color: #0d1b26; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(13, 27, 38, 0.1); transition: box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(13, 27, 38, 0.15); transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-card__eyebrow { font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #2a6b96; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-card__title { font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, sans-serif; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.3; letter-spacing: -0.005em; color: #0d1b26; } .ds-card__meta { font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.5; color: #475569; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Hairline-bordered field. Focus shifts border to brand and adds a 3px ring.
components.css: .ds-input-wrap { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, sans-serif; } .ds-input-label { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #1f3441; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-input { height: 36px; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.91 0.008 240); background: transparent; color: #0d1b26; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 400; transition: border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #94a3b8; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #3a8dc4; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(58, 141, 196, 0.5); }

components.name: Brand Chip
components.description: Pill tag for filter and shortlist states. Brand-50 fill, brand-700 text — never a side-stripe.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; height: 24px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 26px; background: #eef6fb; color: #2a6b96; font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; }

components.name: Live Pulse Indicator
components.description: Signature live-status indicator: pulsing dot + tracked LIVE label. Reserved for active shoots feed and numbers strip.
components.css: .ds-live { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-family: Geist, ui-sans-serif, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.06em; color: #1f3441; } .ds-live__dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: oklch(0.70 0.18 145); animation: ds-live-pulse 1.8s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite; } @keyframes ds-live-pulse { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } 50% { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.92); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-live__dot { animation: none; } }

components.name: Magazine Plate Placeholder
components.description: Designed photography placeholder: brand-tinted block with a radial dot grid + Instrument Serif italic caption. Looks intentional, never broken.
components.css: .ds-plate { position: relative; display: block; aspect-ratio: 3 / 4; width: 240px; border-radius: 18px; background-color: #d6e8f3; background-image: radial-gradient(circle at 1px 1px, rgba(13, 27, 38, 0.08) 1px, transparent 0); background-size: 14px 14px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(13, 27, 38, 0.1); overflow: hidden; } .ds-plate__caption { position: absolute; left: 16px; bottom: 16px; font-family: 'Instrument Serif', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 28px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #2a6b96; }

narrative.overview:
CastFlow is infrastructure for a creative industry — the rail that carries money, contracts, and reputation between casters and artists. The design system reads as serious utility for creative people. Stripe-restraint in the chrome, editorial confidence in the typography, real photography of real talent where photography appears at all. The aesthetic explicitly rejects the two reflexes the casting category trains for: the glamour-coded gatekeeper (Models.com severity) and the bargain-coded marketplace (Fiverr clutter). CastFlow is neither. It is the adult version of this.

The system runs on tinted neutrals with one decisive blue accent (Infrastructure Blue, #3a8dc4) and a sparing warm secondary (Set Light, #f9a26c / #e67e3e) reserved for shimmer-button sweeps and eyebrow callouts. Every neutral carries a 240° hue undertone — pure #fff and pure #000 are banned at the stylesheet level. Surfaces alternate between two tints to pace the page editorially; cards are flat with a hairline ring, never glassmorphic, never elevated by drop-shadow.

Photography is a first-class element when it appears: real artist portraits in featured strips and profile pages, designed brand-tinted 'magazine plates' as honest placeholders elsewhere. Motion is multi-moment (marquees, count-ups, scroll reveals, shimmer sweeps) but every effect earns its space — comprehension first, decoration never.
keyCharacteristics: One decisive brand color (#3a8dc4) carrying ~30% of surface presence through drenched sections + CTAs.
Warm CTA accent (#f9a26c → #e67e3e) deployed sparingly on shimmer buttons and eyebrow ornaments only.
Geist Sans for everything operational, Instrument Serif for one editorial moment per page.
Flat surfaces with hairline rings — no drop-shadow vocabulary, ever.
Pill-shaped controls (26px radius / rounded-4xl) on a 10px base radius scale.
All neutrals tinted toward 240° at low chroma; #fff and #000 are prohibited literals.
Magic UI motion library available (marquee, shimmer, shine, orbit, number-ticker) but disciplined.
Dark mode is defined for future authenticated UI; unused on marketing surfaces.

rules.name: The Decisive Blue Rule
rules.body: Infrastructure Blue is decisive, never decorative. It marks CTAs, focus rings, brand moments, and drenched sections — nothing else. It is never a background wash without intent, never a gradient companion, never present 'for vibe.' The page is mostly neutral; the blue earns every appearance.

rules.name: The No-White, No-Black Rule
rules.body: Pure #ffffff and pure #000000 are prohibited literals. Every neutral carries a 240° hue tint at low chroma. If a future utility references #fff or #000, replace with surface-0 and ink-900 respectively.

rules.name: The Single Saturated Color Rule
rules.body: Infrastructure Blue is the only saturated color used at marketing-page scale. Set Light is a sparing accent, never a co-equal hue. Pastel palettes, neon outlines, and rainbow data-viz are forbidden.

rules.name: The One Italic Rule
rules.body: Instrument Serif italic is reserved for one editorial moment per page. It is never used for body, never used for buttons, never used inside cards. Two italic moments on one screen is one too many.

rules.name: The Sans-Body Rule
rules.body: Body copy is Geist Sans. Serif body type is forbidden. If globals.css still sets font-serif as the body default, that is a regression — fix it before shipping anything new.

rules.name: The Tracked-Label Rule
rules.body: Labels and eyebrows carry +0.04em letter-spacing. This is the institutional-voice signal: confident, slightly typeset, never casual.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: box-shadow is forbidden on cards, buttons, panels, modals, and drawers in their resting state. Glassmorphic blurs are forbidden as decorative defaults. Apparent depth is expressed through surface tint and hairline rings.

rules.name: The Hairline-Only Rule
rules.body: All borders are 1px. Side-stripe accents (a 3px or 4px colored border-left as a 'callout' indicator) are prohibited. If a callout needs emphasis, use a full hairline ring and a brand-50 background fill instead.

dos: Do use #3a8dc4 (brand-500) as the only saturated identity color, and brand-700 (#2a6b96) when blue meets body-size text on white.
Do tint every neutral toward 240° — use surface-0 / surface-50 / ink-* tokens, never raw grays.
Do keep buttons on rounded-4xl (26px pill) and cards on rounded-2xl (18px). The contrast between pill controls and softer surfaces is intentional.
Do restrict Instrument Serif italic to one editorial moment per page — usually inside the hero.
Do use the Shimmer Button for the page's primary CTA only. It is the one allowed warm-color moment at button scale.
Do reserve real artist photography for places where it exists; use the Magazine Plate component (dot-grid brand tint) for honest placeholders elsewhere.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion — the global override in globals.css is non-negotiable.
Do let sections breathe with 96–160px desktop vertical padding, but earn the space with substantial content.
donts: Don't use pure #ffffff or #000000 — they are prohibited literals. Use surface-0 and ink-900.
Don't use side-stripe colored borders (border-left: 4px solid …) as callout accents. Use a full hairline ring and a brand-50 fill instead.
Don't use background-clip: text gradient text. Emphasis is via weight or size, never via gradient fill.
Don't use box-shadow for card or button elevation. Surfaces are flat with hairline rings; depth is tonal.
Don't ship glassmorphic blurs (backdrop-filter) as a default decorative treatment. Reserve for rare, purposeful uses.
Don't lay out three-column 'icon + heading + text' feature grids on the homepage. Editorial maximalism means every section has its own shape — the only allowed card grid is Pricing.

Don't chase the anti-references from PRODUCT.md — Models.com gatekeeper severity, Fiverr/Upwork clutter, gradient-hero SaaS templates, Airbnb consumer warmth, 'AI startup' neon-on-black aesthetics, agency-portfolio parallax-everything.
Don't use serif type for body copy. The font-serif body default in legacy globals.css is a bug; if you see it, fix it.
Don't use stock photography of 'diverse smiling teams in meetings' or AI-generated faces. Real platform talent only; if it's not available, use a Magazine Plate or omit the placement.
Don't write CTAs that say 'Click here' or 'Learn more.' Every CTA is verb-led and specific: 'Post your first shoot', 'Find paid work', 'Browse talent free'.
Don't apply dark mode to public marketing pages. Dark tokens exist for future authenticated UI; the marketing site is light-only.
Don't add a second saturated accent color 'for variety.' Set Light is the only warm accent; everything else is the brand-blue family and tinted neutrals.
